chief_complaint: A 67-year-old Bahraini man presented with confusion and rapid neurological decline.
history: The patient had type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and a prior hemi-colectomy. He presented with acute febrile encephalopathy and a positive SARS-CoV-2 test result. Despite high-dose intravenous methylprednisolone, his condition worsened.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not explicitly stated.
labs: MRI scan demonstrated symmetrical hemorrhagic lesions in the bilateral thalami, pons, and periventricular white matter. Follow-up brain MRI scan showed marked lesion regression.
